subject: Add support for Google Stadia controller w/ rumble (#52)
author: Shig

impact.effect_if_absent: Google Stadia controller will not have force feedback/rumble support

userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref: Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:4881 fef->type = FF_RUMBLE; Main_MiSTer/input.cpp:5228 test_bit(FF_RUMBLE, ff_features) (probed via ioctl(fd, EVIOCGBIT(EV_FF, ...)) at input.cpp:5226)

superseded_by: 24175157b852 (upstream HID: hid-google-stadiaff: add support for Stadia force feedback, 2023-07-16)

notes: Byte-identical backport of upstream commit 24175157b852 (authored 2023-07-16 per `git show -s --format=%ai`, committed 2023-08-14; first landed v6.6). Fork added it 2023-09-23 to MiSTer-v5.15 kernel which lacked the feature. RE-VERIFIED via actual byte-for-byte diff (not just source-grep): `diff -u` of `git show 24175157b852:drivers/hid/hid-google-stadiaff.c` against `git show 9521b003c:drivers/hid/hid-google-stadiaff.c` produced zero output -- the .c file is genuinely byte-identical. The Kconfig entry (HID_GOOGLE_STADIA_FF stanza), Makefile line (obj-$(CONFIG_HID_GOOGLE_STADIA_FF) += hid-google-stadiaff.o), and hid-ids.h define (USB_DEVICE_ID_GOOGLE_STADIA 0x9400) are also textually identical between the two commits' diffs. Device ID is 18d1:9400 (USB_VENDOR_ID_GOOGLE=0x18d1 confirmed at hid-ids.h:562, USB_DEVICE_ID_GOOGLE_STADIA=0x9400 bound at hid-google-stadiaff.c:144). Userspace coupling is generic: Main_MiSTer queries FF_RUMBLE capability and uses standard ff_effect interface — not specific to this driver. CONFIG_HID_GOOGLE_STADIA_FF=y enabled in fork's MiSTer_defconfig and in our current linux.config. Absence silently disables force feedback for this controller only. Escalates to upstream: when/if MiSTer kernel is rebased to 6.18+ this commit becomes redundant. CORRECTION to prior pass: userspace_coupling.main_mister_ref cited input.cpp:302/304, which actually falls inside an unrelated USB-keyboard keycode lookup table, not FF_RUMBLE code -- fixed to the real locations (input.cpp:4881, :5228).
sonnet-verified: corrected -- SHA/ancestry, disposition, and the byte-identical equivalence claim CONFIRMED via an actual `diff -u` of both commits' hid-google-stadiaff.c (zero output) plus Kconfig/Makefile/hid-ids.h; the Main_MiSTer citation was wrong (pointed at an unrelated keycode table) and has been fixed to the real FF_RUMBLE lines (input.cpp:4881, :5228).
